headline.text: L1 S2 enrober wire mesh damaged and replaced — all *pieces accounted for*, new belt installed and ATP-passed.

summary: One active food-safety event resolved: damaged wire mesh in L1 S2 enrober, all pieces recovered and disposed, new mesh installed. Roller wear flagged for follow-up inspection. L3 MFM GMP housekeeping deviations repeated for second time this week.

food_safety.title: Damaged wire mesh in S2 enrober replaced
food_safety.summary: Wire mesh belt stopped turning, found damaged with caramel and almonds inside enrober and mesh; maintenance replaced entire belt, all pieces of damaged mesh accounted for and disposed, new mesh ATP-passed.
food_safety.status: Resolved — new mesh installed at 11:33, ATP passed, line restarted at 14:06.

food_safety.title: S2 roller wear may produce metal shavings
food_safety.summary: Grooves visible on Tunnel 2 roller ends; Curt Willis to evaluate during tomorrow's clean to eliminate risk of metal particles entering product.
food_safety.status: Open, awaiting inspection during next scheduled cleaning.

quality.title: MFM FIFO violation — newest WIP staged
quality.summary: Only 1 pallet of 4/14 date staged for production while older dates remain in staging; team reminded WIP must run first-in, first-out.
quality.status: Open — reminder issued to 3rd shift team.

quality.title: S2 trays with pink discoloration isolated
quality.summary: 19 trays showing pink discoloration on cluster image; team coached not to use them.

quality.title: FFS bad product increase overnight
quality.summary: Zack Kurtz reported elevated reject rate across multiple shifts; no specific root cause identified.

sanitation.title: MFM GMP deviations repeated — second occurrence
sanitation.summary: Waste container without lid and scrap trays on cardboard instead of authorized yellow trays; same deviations corrected earlier this week, recurred on 1st shift.
sanitation.status: Resolved — team leader notified, corrective action taken on shift.

opportunities.text: Consider adding enrober mesh belt and roller visual inspection to S2 startup and weekly PM checklist — based on today's damaged mesh event and roller wear flagged at 19:00.
Review GMP housekeeping verification frequency at MFM — same two deviations (waste lids, tray storage) corrected twice this week, suggesting gap in shift handoff or checklist adherence.
Strengthen FIFO verification at MFM WIP staging — based on newest-date pallet staged ahead of older inventory on 3rd shift.
